displayTitle: Prove the Film Before You Read the Weld

beats.narration: A radiograph is evidence. Before you read the weld, three proofs come first: the I Q I, your eyes, and the identification.

payload.title: PROVE THE FILM
payload.sub: Evidence has to prove its own chain of custody.

beats.narration: The I Q I goes source side, far from the film, enduring full geometric unsharpness. Film side only when unreachable — flagged with a lead F.

payload.heading: Source side first — or flag it
payload.note: NO SOURCE ACCESS → FILM SIDE + LEAD F

beats.narration: The plaque rides a shim beside the weld. Wires lie across it. Panoramic shots carry at least three indicators, a hundred twenty degrees apart.

tiles.s: On a shim, beside the weld

beats.narration: Then check yourself: several minutes of dark adaptation, an annual near vision exam. And the label — permanent identification matching the technique record.

payload.heading: Check yourself, then check the label

beats.narration: So verify the I Q I, verify your eyes, verify the identification — only then say your first word about the weld.

payload.sub: Verify all three — only then start reading.
